name: Henny Youngman's Cheese Blintzes
recipeInstructions:
To make batter, put the eggs, milk, oil, and salt in a blender container. Blend well. Add the flour and blend until just combined, scraping down the sides once.
Brush an 8-inch frying pan with melted butter. Pour about 2 to 3 tbsp of batter into the pan, tilting it so the batter coats the bottom. Cook until the edges begin to turn golden. Shake the pan to loosen the pancake, then carefully turn it out onto a paper towel, browned side up. Repeat with remaining butter and batter, stacking cooked pancakes between sheets of paper towel.
Combine all filling ingredients in a mixing bowl. Arrange a pancake browned side up, and place a heaping spoonful of filling just above the center. Fold the sides toward the middle, overlapping edges, then fold down the top to seal in the filling. Roll the pancake into a neat packet. Arrange blintzes seam side down on a heated griddle or large skillet brushed with butter. Cook until browned, turning once. Makes 14 to 18 blintzes.
